entry.title: Re: How to load prospective data using incremental feature
entry.content:
One method may be to load data to a dummy time period (e.g. "#"), and then use a data action to move the data accordingly. 

Have tested and this seems to work:

DATA([d/Date]=PREVIOUS(3,"DAY",TODAY()))=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date]="#")

You could embed within a multi action sequence. E.g. 1) Import step to "#" date (with clear and replace subset on date/version to avoid data debris) 2) Data action to move data to correct date.

entry.title: Sapnote1644457 - Default item category for components in maintenance and service orders not working
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have set the "Define Default Values for Component Item Categories" in customizing to :

Mat.type = ERSA and Plant to right Plant code.

But still when we add a component to the workorder with that plant code and type ERSA , Still SAP gives an error "Enter an item Category". There is also no user exit used at the moment.

Sap Note : 1644457 - Default item category for components in maintenance and service orders - SAP for Me is already checked of course.

We are on ECC 617 SP26

 

greetings Rob

entry.title: Re: Not able to get Flavour Creation option in SAP Screen Personas
entry.content:
Hi,

Do you have /PERSONAS/EDITOR_ROLE assigned for your user?
Where are you trying to use Personas? Through "sap/bc/personas" service?

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: House Banks in S/4HANA – Change in SAP’s Approach
entry.content:
Hello ,

I am trying to save house bank account details on NWBC tcode, I have entered all the details but while trying to save the data i am getting error data was not saved again and again. 

Can you help me on this error ?

entry.title: Re: Add Header Text in Basic Date of Supplier Invoice
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out!

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the layout of the ‘Create Supplier Invoice’ app, including the placement of fields in the ‘Basic Data’ and ‘Details’ sections, is determined by the standard SAP Fiori design. Unfortunately, it’s not possible to move fields between these sections using the ‘Adapt UI’ feature. This feature allows you to personalize the user interface, but it doesn’t support moving fields between different sections of the app.

Kind Regards, 

Roxana

entry.title: Re: RFC Adapter with Z Remote Functions - Getting Exception "Error Getting Function"
entry.content: Hi, We are also facing same issue ,if you resolve above issue ? Please share your experience while resolving above error. Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Not able to get Flavour Creation option in SAP Screen...
entry.content: Yes

entry.title: SuccessFactors Data Model and Database structure
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am looking for an information what is precisely the structure of data tables lying underneath of XML based foundation objects in SF on the one side, and MDF based foundation objects on the other side. 

In my understanding, XML based objects have three-like data structure with one to one relation and pretty rigid forms, as for MDF based objects the data structure is relational and more flexible (rows, columns). It would be important for me to understand potential impact in a positive and negative sense. It goes even further if i think on how HANA data base is structured and how SF on hana can use (if at all) the way hana is (or supposed to be) structured (column,  indexing etc.). 

Can somebody please help me out regarding and documentation out there (I could not find nothing in SAP Help Portal or KBAs)? Thanks!

Best regards,

Milos

entry.title: Re: Comprehensive Guide on SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Migration
entry.content: Very informative. Thank you, Anmol.

entry.title: CDS View entity union all: take the value of a field from table EINA to table EINE
entry.content:
Hello, I am new to CDS, I want to extract data from two tables in a view: EINA and EINE.

For each EINE line we have an EINA line, the reverse is not true.

To do this I use the UNION ALL statement.

My need is very simple, in the EINA table we have the MATNR field, but not in the EINE table, so in my CDS view I would like for each EINE line to take the associated value from EINA.MATNR to my CDS view.

Here is my example code:

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'EINA/EINEE Union'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}

define view entity ZC_EinaEineUnion as select from eina
{
  key eina.infnr,
  'EINA' as TableSource,
   eina.matnr
}
union all select from eine
{
  key eine.infnr,
  'EINE' as TableSource,
  '' as matnr
}

 

When executed it gives:

My need: in the matnr column, I would like to have for all EINE lines the value '000000000007047609'.

Thank you in advance for your help

We also have a contest open right now and everyone is invited to enter!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Getting an error while trying to run the task list SA...
entry.content: This was happening because STMS configuration was not done. Once STMS configuration was done, the problem was solved.

entry.title: Re: Can't link my IAM Travel App to a published OData service

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Hi, Franzisca from the SAP Field Service Management Mobile team here - we don't actively block 3rd party keyboard to my knowledge. Maybe your users can point out in which iOS App version using a custom keyboard worked? That would help figuring this out.

entry.title: Getting Exception "Error Getting Function" in RFC Adapter with Remote Functions
entry.content:
Hi Expert ,

in CPI tenant, while calling RFC adapter to SAP on-prem ECC with standard BAPI from HTTP endpoint, we are getting expected result whereas when we use custom FM (Remote Enabled) we are getting "Error Getting Function".

How can we solve this issue ?

 

thank you in advance

BR

entry.title: How do I modify the Planning Date on FB01?
entry.content:
Hi, I'm trying to modify the BSEG-FDTAG or the Planning Date on FB01.

When the user sets the payment terms, the days/percent field will be populated. <--- There is an existing logic for this.

I'm trying to do is Sum the Days in the Days/Percent field and Baseline Date.

Example.

Payment terms is 123 and the Days/Percent is 10 days/ 10%.

Baseline Date is July 31, 2024.

Planning date should be about August 10, 2024.

I have a custom program named ZPROGSTEPS that calls a step from GBB1. However, when the step was called, the value of BSEG was empty. Hence, no values were added but when I tried to save the document, the date of the Planning Date was equal to the Baseline Date.

I've tried to set the BSEG-FDTAG = '20250101' in my custom program but unfortunately, when I saved the document, the planning date was still equal to the baseline date.

What is the possible solution here? Should I create a step in GBB1 and call that on my custom program?

entry.title: SAP B1 UI error after upgrade
entry.content:
Hello,

We are having an issue where after upgrading to SAP  B1 v10 from SAP B1 v9.3, some users are having UI issues. Specifically on the Business Partner Master Data - Addresses window, the "text" field is completely missing. This is happening to only a few  specific users after the upgrade. The users have the same issue on  different devices. We tried disabling the addons we use (B1UP) but no luck.

Anyone has experienced this before, or has any suggestion please?

Thank you.

entry.title: BADI - User Exit for Transaction CJ20N Saving Purchase Price at Activity - Network Level
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Here's the requirement

I need to add mark-up % for every item's purchase price in network's activity and save (Ctrl + S ) the project, that's when the Badi or User-Exit must trigger, but having a hard time to find the right User-Exit or BaDi for this task.

 

 

 


Will you please help me out to do this.
Thank you and regards

entry.title: Re: Task 9 - Using CQL in an unbound action (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content: Passed 😀

entry.title: Description field inheritance from a book page template
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm currently creating a new book page template for a cover page.

In this template, I have provided some comments in the description field to remind the authors how to use this template (there are some placeholders to use).

If I use this template as a master copy, its description is inherited in a new created book page. However, I'm not interested in this solution since I will need to add later changes to this template in the future.

Nevertheless, if I use this template as a "real" template (i.e. by setting its subtype to "Book Page Template"), the description field in a newly created book page based on this template seems void.

Is there a way to inherit the description field from this template in a new book page?

entry.title: Adding person responsible in Production Order at operation level
entry.content:
Hi,

There is a requirement to have the user responsible available at Manage work center app in public cloud.

Please suggest what is the option available in S/4HANA public cloud version to have the user responsible added at the operation level.


Regards

Narendra

entry.title: Re: purchase requisition document "External Processing status"

entry.title: Check the Availability of Your Multi-Environment HANA Cloud Instances with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In this blog post, I share hands-on insights on how to minimize downtime for your SAP HANA Cloud database by leveraging SAP Automation Pilot. By simply going through this step-by-step guide, you will learn how easy it is to set up SAP Automation Pilot to to perform automated availability checks effortlessly, so that you minimize disruptions, keep your business running smoothly and maximize performance!

Introduction

Undoubtedly, ensuring that your HANA Cloud instances are always up and running can be crucial for the success of your business operations. In Biser Simeonov's last blog post co-authored by me, “Automated Availability Check for SAP HANA Cloud (on CF level) with SAP Automation Pilot”, we showed you how to automate the monitoring of your HANA Cloud instances enabled in a Cloud Foundry space by using SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP – two powerful tools from the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) DevOps portfolio. You learned how to utilize these tools to check the availability of your instances, and even start the ones that are not accessible. So far so good!

However, with the 2022 QRC3 release in October 2022, SAP HANA Cloud went "multi-environment". As a result, now you can have HANA Cloud instances in subaccounts that don’t have the Cloud Foundry environment enabled. For more information, see:

  • Subscribing to the SAP HANA Cloud Administration Tools (Multi-Environment)
  • SAP HANA Cloud goes "multi-environment": Part 1- feature overview

In the following guide, I will show you how to perform the same HANA Cloud Availability Check for HANA Cloud instances enabled in “Other Environments” (multi-environment setup).

How does the HANA Cloud Availability Check work?

The process is visualized in the graphic below. I advise you to check out the previous blog post for a more detailed explanation.

Step-by-Step Guide

Prerequisites

  • SAP HANA Cloud database provisioned on subaccount level (multi-environment);
  • SAP Automation Pilot (See Initial Setup);
  • SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP enabled in Cloud Foundry (See Initial Setup);
  • Integration between SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP (See Integrate with SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP);
  • SAP Service Manager instance with plan “subaccount-admin” provisioned on space level or on subaccount level;

After you’ve made sure that you meet all the prerequisites, you can proceed with the steps below.

1. Verify that you have provisioned SAP Automation Pilot. You should be able to see it in the “Subscriptions” section in your subaccount:

NOTE: SAP Automation Pilot can interact with resources in any BTP landscapes. All you need is to have at least one instance of SAP Automation Pilot.

2. Verify that you have Alert Notification and Service Manager instances, and that your HANA Cloud instance is enabled in “Other Environments”.

Your Service Manager instance can be provisioned either in your subaccount, or in your space. The only difference this will make is whether you will have to create a “Service Binding” or a “Service Key” later.

3. You need to create a Service Key for your Alert Notification service instance. This key will be used on a later stage by the SAP Automation Pilot command. To create a Service Key, choose the “Create Service Key” option:

Enter a name for your service key and choose “Create”:

4. Next, you need to create a “Service Binding” for your HANA Cloud instance, which will be used by the SAP Automation Pilot command. To do that, choose the “Create Service Binding” option:

Enter a name for your service binding and choose “Create”:

5. Depending on the scope of your Service Manager instance, you need to create:

  • a “Service Key” if your instance is on space level.
  • a “Service Binding” if your instance is on subaccount level.

6. The SAP Automation Pilot team has prepared a catalog with example commands, which can be imported directly into your SAP Automation Pilot instance.

Copy the contents of the catalog:

7. Now you need to import the catalog with example commands in your SAP Automation Pilot instance. From the left-side menu, choose “My Catalogs” and then “Import”:

Paste the catalog and choose “Import”:

8. You can see the imported catalog “Automation Pilot Examples” in “My Catalogs”. From this catalog, choose “Commands”:

9. From the catalog, open the “CheckHanaCloudMultiEnvAvailabilitySample” command by clicking on it. Explore the Input Keys, the Output Keys, and the Executors of the command:

10. Scroll down to “Configuration” and choose the “notifyANS” executor. You need to define the event that SAP Automation Pilot will ingest into Alert Notification service. Choose “Edit” to edit the properties of your event:

You can use these example values for the required event properties of your event:

  • body: The availability check for HANA Cloud with Service Instance ID $(.execution.input.hanaServiceInstance) has failed. Now the Automation Pilot proceeds with an automated START for this HANA Cloud instance.
  • category: ALERT
  • eventType: FailedHCAvailabilityCheck
  • resourceName: hana
  • resourceType: database
  • serviceKey: $(.execution.input.AnsServiceKey) -> use a dynamic expression to get the AnsServiceKey from the inputs
  • severity: ERROR
  • subject: Failed HANA Cloud Availability Check $(nowMillis | toDate("yyyy-MM-dd"))

11. After you have done that, trigger the command:

12. You need to provide values for the following input keys:

  • AnsServiceKey - Service Key for your Alert Notification service instance.
  • hanaClientTokenURL - The URL needed to be called in order to generate a token to access your HANA Cloud instance. The URL is built as follows: in the HANA Cloud service binding file, in the "uaa" section, look for the specified "url" + add to it /oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials.
  • hanaCloudBindingKey - Service Binding for your Hana Cloud instance
  • hanaCloudResourceName - The exact name of your HANA Cloud instance, e.g. "hanaDemo", "hanaProd", etc.
  • hanaCluster - Needed to access the HANA Cloud API gateway, where 'cluster' typically identifies either a production, pre-production or trial environment.
  • hanaDatacenter - Needed to access the HANA Cloud API gateway, where 'datacenter' is the data center where your HANA Cloud is deployed, i.e. eu10, us10, etc.
  • hanaServiceInstance - The Instance ID of your HANA Cloud instance - it can be found from the provisioned service in BTP Cockpit, look for "Instance ID".
  • ServiceManagerKey - Service Key or Service Binding for your Service Manager instance used to perform management tasks against multiple instances in your BTP subaccount.

You can also look at the prepared outputs (no need to provide values for them but it is important to understand what data is returned as the command's output):

  • checkPing - The entire response for the metric "HDBAccessible" returned by HANA Cloud Metrics Service REST API.
  • isHanaCloudAvailable - Check on whether the HANA Cloud instance is available, where: "true" = HANA Cloud is available; "false" = HANA Cloud is not available.
  • resultHanaStart - Check on whether the HANA Cloud instance has been started successfully, where: "true" = HANA Cloud has been started; "false" = HANA Cloud was not started'.

13. Provide values for all input keys and then trigger the command:

There are two possible options for the outcome of the execution:

Option 1: Your HANA Cloud instance is accessible and the command for starting HANA Cloud has not been triggered. You should be able to see the following execution details:

If you click on “Output Values”, you can see the output values for checkPing, isHanaCloudAvailable, and resultHanaStart:

You can verify in SAP Hana Cloud Central that your HANA Cloud instance is up and running:

Option 2: Your HANA Cloud instance is not accessible and the command for starting HANA Cloud has been triggered:

You can verify in SAP Hana Cloud Central that your HANA Cloud instance is starting:

If you click on “Output Values”, you can see the output values for checkPing, isHanaCloudAvailable, and resultHanaStart:

You have successfully performed the HANA Cloud Availability Check via SAP Automation Pilot. Now you can make a subscription in Alert Notification service, so that you can receive a notification when SAP Automation Pilot detects that your multi-environment HANA Cloud instance is not accessible. If you want to do that, continue with the following steps:

14. Navigate to your Alert Notification service instance and open it. Choose the “Subscriptions” tab form the left-side menu and then “Create” to create a new subscription:

15. Enter a name for your subscription and click “Create” to move on to the next step:

16. Next, create a Condition. Give your condition a name. Then define your condition by choosing the event property “eventType”, operand “Is Equal To”, and, in our case, expected value “FailedHCAvailabilityCheck”:

The expected value for “eventType” is the one you provided in step 10:

17. To finish your subscription in Alert Notification service, select an Action by choosing “Create Action”:

Select an Action Type of your preference, for example “Email” and choose “Next”:

Give your Action a name, and then provide the email address where you want to receive the email notification:

NOTE: After you have created some of the actions, they are labeled as Not Confirmed. To enable them, you have to confirm them as described in Confirming Actions.

Choose “Save” to finish making your subscription. You will see a confirmation that your subscription was created:

You can find more information about Subscriptions, Conditions, and Actions in Configuration Management Using the SAP BTP Cockpit.

18. Now you can test your subscription. If you stop your HANA Cloud Instance and trigger the CheckHanaCloudMultiEnvAvailabilitySample command again, you will receive an email from Alert Notification service with information that the HANA Cloud Availability Check has failed. You can see an example email below:

NOTE: If you also want to receive notifications in a Microsoft Teams channel of your choice, check out the previous blog post for a detailed step-by-step explanation on how to make this configuration.

Wrap-up

In this blog, you've learned to utilize SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP to effectively monitor the availability of your multi-environment HANA Cloud instances. By using these innovative tools in your daily operations, you can save time, prevent potential disruptions, and ensure your operations continue to run smoothly. If you are interested to learn more about these products and how they can transform your business, please refer to the following sources:

SAP Automation Pilot:

  • SAP Discovery Center;
  • Help documentation;
  • Public GitHub Automation Pilot sample use cases;

SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP:

  • SAP Discovery Center;
  • Help documentation

You can also check out these very useful tutorials prepared by our colleague Dan van Leeuwen:

  • Alerts in SAP HANA Database and Data Lake
  • Automating SAP HANA Cloud Tasks with the SAP Automation Pilot Service

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q4-23 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Hi @BrunoWillequet, this is currently not possible as supplier and business partner (=transport provider) are separate entities. The consolidation happens on the product level.

Best, Nico

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check - How to do it right.
entry.content:
Hi Steven,
to add to Gerald's answer, RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA is the collector job for SAP Readiness Check. Actually, it subsequently starts /SDF/RC_START_CHECK to get the simplification item check results.

Thus, the results should be the same, at least when executed at the same point in time and using the same ST03N usage data (e.g. from production system). 

However, because we designed SAP Readiness Check as general planning tool for upgrades or conversions in the first place, the results are displayed differently. In Readiness Check you will find one tile with simplification items describing immediate upgrade change impacts and another one for simplification items related compatibility packages while in the ALV view of /SDF/RC_START_CHECK all is mixed and need to manually filter them.

Generally, /SDF/RC_START_CHECK you should more use for the immediate preparation of the technical system upgrade in addition to the calls by SUM to make sure no consistency check error message for the upgrade exist. 

Regards,
Stefan Berndt

entry.title: Re: FO has the balance amount but still showing target va...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out! 

Please see the following SAP note that explains the standard system behavior for  setting up the delivery indicator for Service Purchase Orders & SES:

KInd Regards, 

Roxana

entry.title: How to configure the Original language of an SAP workflow?
entry.content:
I am trying to edit a standard workflow but it is not editable. Whenever I click on the edit button it is giving a message that "Object maintenance in this system only possible in original language". Usually the message specifies the original language in this message, but in this one it is not specified. I tried to log in German and Russian as well but could not make it editable. 

I would appreciate any help and suggestion.

entry.title: Re: Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) - Introduction
entry.content:
I have a Sales Order which has a provisional price while creating the sales order. By the time when the invoice will be done I will have the final price. Will CPE automatically reprice and update the price in the sales order with the latest quotation? Or this is a manual activity which the user needs to do? How to handle situations like this in CPE. 

Regards

MM

entry.title: SQLAnywhere17ThirdPartyLegal.pdf and Java Runtime License
entry.content:
The document SQLAnywhere17ThirdPartyLegal.pdf is not mentioning the Java Runtime SAPJVM probably because it is an integral part of the product. Anyway some customers ask for clarification as Java licenses are audited. Please advise where to find a documented reference that the Java runtime is part of the SQLA license?

Especially as the COPYRIGHT file inside the JRE directory says "Copyright © 1993, 2023, Oracle and/or its affiliates." Onyl the buildinfo.txt refers to brand=sapjvm

entry.title: Re: How to extract XSD from a WSDL with multiple Schema definitions
entry.content: @vinaymittal Most of people won't read your comment but you may edit your blog post and add a warning at the top.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW - Master Data Delta Load
entry.content:
As i can see you have enhanced master data for one of the field using CMOD so in this case you need to follow below steps :

  • Before moving changes to production system its better to clear all delta Q ( RSA7 ) through the delta info package 
  • Once you move your changes to production system then reinitionalise the delta with "with data transfer" option so that you will get historical data for the enahnced field as well as if you missed any records that will come through full load using this option.

If you satisfied with this solution please mark as correct answer so that it will help to many peoples having this conserns.

Regards,

Ganesh

Have you checked these configurations?

entry.title: Approval Mobile Notification
entry.content:
Hi,

We recently implemented approval for Purchase Orders in SAP B1. However, we're encountering an issue where some approvers can access SAP B1 on their mobile devices, but the Approval window appears empty. Despite this, they are receiving notifications when they log in to SAP B1. We have already checked the Preferences on their mobile devices.

Has anyone experienced this issue before? Were you able to resolve it? If so, please share your solution.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Sapnote1644457 - Default item category for components in maintenance and service orders not work
entry.content:
Greetings. Can you share a screen shot of your customizing:

Plant Maintenance and Customer Service > Maintenance and Service Processing > Maintenance and Service Orders > General Data > Define Default Values for Component Item Categories

Also a screen shot of your maintenance order.

entry.title: Creating an Email Destination in SAP Build Process Automation: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Step 1: Navigate to Your BPA Tenant

Log in to your SAP BPA environment. Ensure you have the appropriate permissions to manage destinations.

Step 2: Open Control Tower

From the main menu, navigate to the Control Tower. 

Step 3: Click on Destinations

In the Control Tower, find and click on Destinations. This section allows you to manage various types of destinations.

Step 4: Click Open BTP Cockpit

To create a new destination, click on the Open BTP Cockpit button. This will redirect you to the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) Cockpit.

Step 5: Create Destination

  1. In the BTP Cockpit, click on Create Destination.
  2. Enter the details for your new email destination:

    Additional properties

    mail.smtp.from : <your email id>
    mail.transport.protocol=smtp
    mail.smtp.host=smtp.gmail.com
    mail.smtp.starttls.enable=true
    mail.smtp.starttls.required=true
    mail.smtp.port=587
    mail.smtp.auth=true

     
    • Name: sap_process_automation_mail (keep the name exactly in this format)
    • Type: Select Mail from the dropdown menu.
    • Description: Optionally, add a brief description.
    • Proxy Type: Select the appropriate proxy type (usually "Internet").
    • Authentication: Choose Basic Authentication.
    • User: Enter your email address.
    • Password: Enter the app password generated for your email account (details below)

Creating an App Password for Gmail

If you are using a Gmail account, you need to create an app password instead of using your regular email password. Follow these steps to generate an app password:

  1. Go to your Gmail account and click on Manage Your Google Account.
  2. Navigate to the Security section.
  3. Under How you sign in to Google, click on 2-Step Verification.
  4. Click on App Passwords.
  5. Give an App name. 
  6. Click Create. A password will be generated on your screen.
  7. Copy the generated password and use it in the Password section when configuring the email destination.

Step 6: Save the Destination

Once you have entered all the required details, click Save to create the destination. 

Now that you have created the email destination, you can use it in your workflows to send automated emails.

entry.title: Re: Setting up a UI5 Application on BAS for CAPM Full Stack UI5 Development
entry.content: hi, nice guide, but i have done all the steps but couldn't get the oData in the the frontEnd, the oData is null.

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Advanced Event Mesh or APIM when to use what?
entry.content:
hi dop,

Thank you for your response! In our landscape we are using other APIM tools(like Apigee,Kong gateway etc.,) for API interfaces and SAP Advanced Event Mesh topics are driven by us. Could you please let me know factors that should be considered for choosing Advanced Event Mesh? Should we refer ISA-M offered by SAP before we decide which integration pattern to choose for what use case? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: WOCR Determination in SAP EWM Based on Specific Requirement- Part 1
entry.content:
Hello,

many thx for work. I need some lession concerning about the theme.

I would be very happy if you can make a description of stock order creation rules that first full HUs and then a partial quantity of an HU is withdrawn by means of a pick. There is no packing specification because each HU can have a different quantity of the article. I'll be honest, I was supposed to set it up for a customer this year and failed

entry.title: Why does the system accept Order Number which is out of range defined in KONK?
entry.content:
Why does the system accept Order Number which is out of range defined in KONK?

Eg. I create in KO01 new Order: RMTESTTEST (10 characters and more) and it is acceptable by the system. While the number range in KONK for this type of orders is: RMAAAAAAA - RMZZZZZZZ  (9 characters) ?

Why don't I get any warning and can create the order ?

I work on SAP ERP 6.0

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
@JP,

If I got it right, you could test the following mock up:

  1. Begin with a GRPO for your item at $100.
  2. Add a Landed Cost for the standard freight cost of $20, allocating it to the “Freight” cost line.
  3. Later, the actual cost increases to $30 (an additional $10), so, set up a new Landed Cost type called “Freight Variance” and create a new Landed Cost based on the original one, and allocate the $10 to it.
  4. Finally, generate the AP Invoice for the Freight Carrier. Use the “Copy From > Landed Costs” option, which will include both the original Freight cost and the Freight Variance as services.

This process ensures traceability, as all related documents are linked and visible on the relationship map. The result will be similar to this:

entry.title: SAP CodeJam in Vienna 2024
entry.content:
Dear CodeJam Team,

the SAP Community in Vienna would like to organize a CodeJam, these are the cornerstones:
Date: 08.11.2024 (we are organizing a SIT on the day after)
Location: we have a Location that can accomodate about 25 people
Preferred Topic: ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
Contact: hello@sit.wien

We hope that's possible with your schedule and we'll be happy to hear from you.

Kind regards,
SAP Community Vienna Orga Team

If you still get error 403. Maybe a little but important detail is missing regarding content modifier 2 in the iflow: fetching the x-csrf token:

entry.title: Re: Solr upgrade issue with version 8.1 to 9.2 in Commerc...
entry.content:
By mistake I have updated the version property value in hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\resources\solr\9.2\customizations/meta.properties then configurations mismatched with OOTB - solrserver.solr.customizations.versions. I rectified now, it's working fine.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Check the Availability of Your Multi-Environment HANA Cloud Instances with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content: Great article and shared hands-on experience!

entry.title: SAP AEM requirement and guidance
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement where when ever "Material Number" is getting updated on sender system(we) then event should be sent with only "Material Number" to receiver partner system.. Then the receiver partner system makes an API call with "Material Number" value as input to us(sending system) and then as a response to that API call updated price info(plus other fields) should be sent back as API response..

In this case, Advanced Event Mesh(AEM) comes in to picture only in case of sending "Material Number" when ever there is a change right? Does sending notification event makes sense when there is only one field value to be sent to the partner system? Should we choose Notification event over data event in this case? I have experience on configuration of Queues/topics in AEM for Data event but not for notification event..  Could you please let me know if there is any difference in terms of configuration in AEM for Data Vs Notification event?

Also most importantly, should we ask receiving partner system to develop an API on their side to consume the "Material Number" value (or) should they pull and consume the data via AMQP protocol? (or) what do you propose in this scenario to establish stable interface which is cost minimal solution as well?

Regards!

entry.title: Re: MSS On-Behalf Maintain Time Events is inactive
entry.content: I solved it on my own. There was a missing Target-Mapping

entry.title: Re: Data Source and Service Selection configuration in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@Alexander_Schaedlich , Thanks. with the above information you was able to successfully actiavte the booster - ABAP Environment in SAP BTP and connected to ADT - Ecplise. You need to generate/confiure a HTTP Services. (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-ZxpAt8QgfM) 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/http-services-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13530685 

After successful service confifuration in ADT, you can able to select the services when working in BAS. 

Hope this helps. Thanks. 

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Vienna 2024
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

we are happy to announce the SAP Inside Track Vienna 2024. The event will take place on 09.11.2024. This event will be a great opportunity to join up with people of the SAP Community, whether you are a SAP User, Developer or Consultant, SAP-affiliated or not.

The Call for Speakers will open at the end of July, further details will follow.

If you have any questions, contributions or suggestions to make this a great event, please don't hesitate to contact us at hello@sit.wien

Kind regards,
sitVIE orga team

entry.title: Re: Failed to resolve endpoint: xslt://pd:HCIOWNERPID:MAP...
entry.content:
Resolution

To change the Default System ID configuration to your ERP system id, follow the steps below:

  1. Login to your SAP Business Network account as a Buyer
  2. Navigate to Administration > Configuration > Business Application IDs and End Points (cXML and OData Setup)
  3. You will see Configure Business Application IDs screen > Click in the Default System ID
  4. Rename it to your ERP System ID by changing the System ID value
  5. Click Save

In the Configuration page, click SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network:

  1. Uncheck the Enable SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network check box
  2. Click Save
  3. In the Configuration page, click SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network
  4. Check the Enable SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network check box
  5. Click Save

entry.title: Wily Introscope High availability setup
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We are upgrading our Solution Manager to 7.2 SP 18, and in the process, we are also updating our CA Introscope version to 10.8.

We have setup high availability following the below procedure from Broadcom: What are the steps to implement failover and High Availability (HA) MOMs? (broadcom.com)

This is working as expected, excluding the DNS part.

However, when trying to connect the Introscope to, we are installing the DAA agent using Physical hostname of the server and the same is used when detecting the Introscope installation in Solution manager "Infrastructure configuration" step.

In the above case, please let us know how we can setup the high availability environment for CA Introscope, considering the functionality given in the link shared above.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Wily Introscope High availability setup
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Please follow High Availability Setup for Introscope Enterprise Manager. This documentation is available under the following link: High Availability Setup for Introscope Enterprise Manager (sap.com) 

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Restrict few SAP Fiori Notification to SAP Workzone and Mobile Devices
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have configured multiple notifications in the SAP S/4 system. This included configuring the standard notification as well as custom notification.

I have also integrated the SAP Workzone notification with the SAP Fiori Launchpad notification and configured the SAP Mobile Start to get the notification on mobile devices.

My question is how we can restrict some notifications to Fiori launchpad only and not pass that notification to SAP Workzone or mobile devices. 

Is there any way which is provided by the SAP Notification framework by which we can do this so that my sensitive notification can only be on Fiori launchpad and not move to Workzone or mobile devices?

entry.title: Terraform Provider for SAP BTP version 1.5.0 - what’s new
entry.content:
Here we go again – welcome 1.5.0

The July release aka version 1.5.0 of the Terraform provider for SAP BTP is out. Let’s look at what has been added since the last release.

Of course, you also find all the information in the release notes in the GitHub repository:

Feature: Increased timeout for resource subaccount subscription

Some users might have run into a time-out issue when provisioning a subscription on subaccount level. While this should be a rare case, we provided a quick fix and increased the default timeout for this resource to 60 minutes.

This is a temporal fix, and we will provide a timeout block for the resource in the upcoming release as you already know from environment and service instances.

Feature: Support for subaccount service broker

The new release contains a new resource as well as the corresponding data sources for service brokers on subaccount level.

You find details on how to use them in the documentation:

  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_service_broker
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/data-sources/subaccount_service_broker
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/data-sources/subaccount_service_brokers

Feature: Resource to create roles

While the Terraform provider supported data sources for roles, you could not provision them up to now. With release 1.5.0 we provide a resource that allows you to do exactly that namely provision roles via Terraform on SAP BTP. The resource is available on global account, directory, and subaccount level.

As usual you find the details on parameters and examples in the documentation:

  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/globalaccount_role
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/directory_role
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_role

Fix: Enable creation of duplicate service instances in different environments

You might have run into an issue when trying to service instances with the same name in different environments e.g., one in Cloud Foundry and one on BTP in the runtime “other”.

In case you tried this, you have gotten an error stating “BadRequest”.

We had a small glitch in the data retrieval of the service instance resource that was fixed in this release, so this issue is gone.

Fix: Check for multiple auth parameters

Providing the authentication data for the provider can be done via variables in your configuration or via environment variables. We had some gaps comparing the checks via the provider schema and the injection via environment variables. This would have resulted in “strange” errors messages on your end that were hard to pin down.

These gaps are closed, and the validation now has you covered with meaningful error messages.

Improvement: Improved error handling for entitlement destruction

Sometimes things can go sideways when provisioning or deprovisioning your resources. In these cases, error messages that help you to find the root cause are important.

We are continually improving the provider in this area. An example scenario for this is when you try to destroy an entitlement for which a service instance exists outside of Terraform's lifecycle.

With this release we added more context to the error message that you get when deleting an entitlement that should help you in investigating the reasons for the issue.

How to update?

The update of the provider version is straightforward. Depending on your configuration in the provider, you must adjust the version constraint to 1.3.0 and execute a “terraform init -upgrade”.

That’s it.

You find the documentation of the enhanced resources in the Terraform registry: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest

Feedback, questions, bugs, feature requests

The procedure hasn’t change: if you want to interact with us, use the GitHub repository https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp:

  • To file a bug, please open an issue in the repository.
  • To file a feature request, please file it in the repository.
  • If you have a question, want to discuss ideas, or do some “show & tell”, feel free to start a GitHub Discussion.

There are also some feature requests open that you can vote for. Check for the issues that are label as “enhancement” and “pending decision“ via this link https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Aenhancement+label%3Apending-decision.

What is next?

Do you want a sneak peek at what is coming next and when we plan to deliver the next release? You find that information in the milestones on our GitHub repository https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/milestones.

Be aware that plans and dates might change through the course of development.

With that … happy Terraforming!

entry.title: Santiago - SAP Stammtisch 12.8.2024 18:00 CLT #sapcodejamslatam
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Santiago has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

SAP Stammtisch in Santiago will happen on August 12th, 2024 at 18:00 CLT for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", we have a table reservation for 12 at the Fuente Chilena.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates South America Roadshow

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2024 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇨🇱 🇦🇷 🇨🇴 (August 8th - 21st 2024)
entry.content: Let's try one SAP CodeJam in October 2024, which is included in SAP Inside Track Fortaleza. We can support the logistics questions!

entry.title: Re: Sapnote1644457 - Default item category for components...
entry.content: Hi , yes as you can see in my explanation it is set. The note to set "Define Default Values for Component Item Categories" is not working for us.

entry.title: Modification of standard SAP Notification
entry.content:
I have activated some of the standard notifications for workflow and purchase orders.

But I have a requirement that the notification text should be changed, and we have to disable some action buttons and have to introduce a new action button. Is there any way in the extended framework for notification by which we can enhance the standard notification?

If we have to restrict certain notifications to only get triggered for some type of business document or some purchase organization, how can we achieve this?

entry.title: SAP Asset Manager - Requested object &ESRT_IDP_ID& is currently locked by user
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While creating a notification in SAM application for all equipments we are getting the below error:

  1. Requested object &ESRT_IDP_ID& is currently locked by user
  2. Error while sending request to backend

We have recently upgraded SAP to 2023 version and the version of SAM we are using is 2110 and also we are using standard SAM client downloaded from Play store/App store.

Device : Iphone 14 pro

Model: 17.5.1(Latest)

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: Bogota - SAP Stammtisch 20.8.2024 18:00 COT #sapcodejamslatam
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Bogota has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

SAP Stammtisch in Bogota will happen on August 20th, 2024 at 18:00 COT for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", we have a table reservation for 12 at the BBC Cerveceria that's in the W Hotel @ Avenida Carrera 9 #115 - 30, Bogota 110111 Colombia.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates South America Roadshow

entry.title: select where condition
entry.content:
SELECT * FROM /scwm/huhdr INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tab) WHERE lgnum = @PA_lgnum.
LOOP AT lt_tab INTO DATA(ls_tab).
  IF ls_tab-hu_guid_ext <> ls_tab-guid_hu_top.
    WRITE: ls_tab-huident.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

 

 

Hi friends,

Can i combine IF ls_tab-hu_guid_ext <> ls_tab-guid_hu_top ( which is in the loop ) in select where condition ?

Thanks,

Madhu M V

entry.title: Re: RFR in SAP RAR
entry.content: @Vinoth747 The program is used for cancelling a POB  when the status in the POB and the SO item level are not the same.

entry.title: Re: Task 9 - Using CQL in an unbound action (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content:
Hi DJ, @qmacro ,

Looks like the instructions inter-twined between action and function.

Did build a function and couldnt get pass the tester changed it to action and it works...

{
ID: "e373bede-7a34-467c-a988-99a0017b708d",
task: "northbreeze-selectproduct",
result: "PASS",
createdAt: "2024-07-26T11:28:27.792Z",
createdBy: "anonymous",
modifiedAt: "2024-07-26T11:28:27.792Z",
modifiedBy: "anonymous",
serviceurl: "https://northbreeze-main.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/odata/v4/northbreeze",
communityid: "cguttikonda24"
},

 Just wondering is there a fluent api approach on select to get count of records from a table.

entry.title: Re: SAP BUILD PROCESS AUTOMATION GETTING ERROR 500 IN RBSC PORTAL FOR AGENT UPDATE
entry.content: Anyone?

entry.title: Re: Advanced Event Mesh or APIM when to use what?
entry.content:
Hi Rama,

 

ISA-M is definitely a good starting point.

As you say, it all depends on the use case. Please contact me via LinkedIn if you have follow up questions.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA DATABASE - SYSTEM USER - Best practices says to deactivate SYSTEM ID
entry.content:
Hello,

it depends on your business scenario. this is the same discussion like with SAP* user in 000 client. System user is only used for maintenance tasks (update e.g.) , for regular tasks you can create dedicated user for your admins with necessary roles (copy system user e.g. )

br

entry.title: Re: Auto packaging in outbound delivery through abap code
entry.content: I achieved it by BDC record and using a function with the IN BACKGROUND TASK DESTINATION 'NONE' statement.

entry.title: Re: Fiori My Paystubs V3 download button not displaying on Android devices
entry.content: I was told by SAP support that the download button only shows for iPhone (iOS).
